title: cota tinctoria var. pallida
intro: Cota tinctoria var. pallida, also known as Dyers Woad or Alpine Cota, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is known to offer various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Cota tinctoria var. pallida can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
